Ground Sessions
01 / 12
A Change Please platform · working title

Ground
Sessions

Live music, recorded properly, in shops that are standing empty. Change Please trainees run the coffee bar. Abbey Road handles the sound. The films go on YouTube. The money goes to ending homelessness.

Scroll / ↓
01 · The idea

Three kinds of overlooked talent.
One room.

01

Prime ground-floor units sit empty across London, New York, LA and Paris while landlords wait for tenants.

02

For every headliner there are a hundred rising acts one good moment away from an audience that stays.

03

Change Please trains people who've experienced homelessness to become baristas, and into jobs.

02 · The format

How one
session works

01

Take an empty unit

Sourced through landlord partners who already open their doors to us.

02

Change Please moves in

A working coffee bar, run by trainees and graduates.

03

An artist records live

One room, one take, stripped back.

04

Abbey Road does the sound

Mobile recording on the night; mix and master after.

05

The film goes out as a series

Artist's name and "recorded by Abbey Road" on every episode.

06

The room earns

Ticketed nights, plus private experience days for corporate partners.

03 · Why it's buildable

The two hardest parts
are already handled

One family, one deal

Abbey Road belongs to Universal.

Talent and sound come from the same company. One partnership covers developing artists, the recording, and the label's marketing reach, instead of two negotiations pulling in different directions.

Space, pre-sorted

The doors are already open.

Change Please already works with Crown Estate, Lendlease and other landlords, and can access vacant units through them. The step that stops most projects like this is the step we start past.

04 · The value exchange

Who brings what.
Who gets what.

What each partner brings Where the income lands

Every line that carries money points at Change Please. The charity sits at the centre of the model, not in a footnote.

Change Please

Brings: baristas, the mission. Gets: income, jobs, profile.

Universal Music

Brings: artists, marketing reach. Gets: launches, streams, press.

Abbey Road

Brings: recording, mix, the name. Gets: new audiences.

Landlords

Bring: empty units. Get: footfall, PR, rates relief.

Sponsors + audience

Bring: funding, tickets, donations. Get: access, impact, member value.

Where the money landsAll income routes back to Change Please.
05 · The lineup

20% names you know.
80% names you're about to.

Curated with Abbey Road. Established acts bring the spike: press, reach, credibility. Rising acts bring the pipeline, and they're the ones a night like this can actually change things for.

It's the same bet Change Please makes on people: back the ones nobody's noticed yet, give them a room, let them show what they can do. Rising artists and rising people on the same stage, the same night.

06 · The money

Two layers.
It matters which pays for what.

Layer 1: pays for production

A founding partner underwrites each city. Corporate experience days are the profit line.

Private session nights for a bank, a card network or an experience company: booked for clients and members, priced properly, proceeds to the charity.

Layer 2: drives the cause and the crowd

Coffee, tickets and donations.

They fund impact and fill the room. They are not asked to cover production. A finished session film costs real money to make, and coffee margins won't cover it. The model is honest about that.

07 · How it grows

The channel grows on
the artists' reach

×1

Artists share their own episode

Each act posts the film to their followers. That's the distribution, bigger than any ad spend we could match.

×2

Universal amplifies

Label channels, playlists, press: written into the deal as a deliverable, not left to goodwill.

×3

A story others can't copy

The person on the machine was homeless six months ago. No other session series has that.

08 · The point

What it's
actually for

Change Please exists to get people out of homelessness through work. Ground Sessions gives that a stage: graduates serving major artists in rooms that were dark a week ago.

Taking part is opt-in and supported: a milestone in someone's programme, never a stunt. And every income line routes back to jobs, training, and a way out.

09 · Where

Four cities. One at a time.

London first: home turf, Abbey Road nearby, the closest Universal relationships. Each new city launches only with its own local underwriter. Different licensing, unions and markets make a simultaneous launch a bad idea.

First: pilot

London

Prove the format, the film, and the corporate day.

Then

New York

Local partner funds the city before doors open.

Then

Los Angeles

Label town; the film library keeps compounding.

Then

Paris

Same playbook, local team.

10 · The plan

Four phases

Phase 0

Lock the architecture

Universal MOU (talent + Abbey Road + amplification). One founding underwriter. Rights and revenue framework. Trainee participation plan built with the programme team.

Phase 1

London pilot

One unit, licences cleared, lean fit-out. Three or four sessions filmed and finished. One or two corporate days. Measure everything.

Phase 2

Build the playbook

Case study with real numbers. Template the deal, the sponsorship, the experience day, the ops checklist.

Phase 3

City by city

New York, LA, Paris, each with a local underwriter. The film library compounds.

11 · What has to go right

Five conditions

01

Artist cross-promotion written into the Universal deal. Without their reach, the channel stays small.

02

A founding underwriter before scale. Coffee and tickets can't fund production.

03

Transparent money flows. The charity at the centre, visibly.

04

Trainee wellbeing designed in. Opt-in, supported, a milestone.

05

London proven before anywhere else.

Empty rooms. New talent. Second chances. Press record.
Change Please × Universal / Abbey Road · contact + next steps
